summaryText: Guest Speaker Leo Bigger opened by sharing his personal story of his mother's passing, using it to illustrate the sustaining hope found in Christ during life's unpredictable seasons. He taught from Galatians 2:20 and Colossians 3:3, emphasizing the profound truth that Christ lives in the believer and the believer's life is hidden with Christ in God. This dual reality provides an unshakable internal hope and external protection. Pastor Leo acknowledged that challenges, attacks from the enemy, and difficult 'valley' seasons are a normal part of the Christian journey, often arising when we are in transition or weak. He warned against the dangers of comparison and jealousy, encouraging listeners to embrace their unique God-given story and calling. The message concluded with a powerful declaration of identity in Christ: because He lives in us, trials, people, money, haters, and demons cannot ultimately defeat us. Pastor Leo closed with a prayer and a prophetic word, encouraging the church to trust God for specific miracles and to fight their battles from the secure position of being in Christ.
mainPoints: Our unshakable hope in hard times is found in the dual reality that Christ lives in us and our lives are hidden with Christ in God.
Do not compare your journey or calling with others; embrace your unique story and trust God's sovereign guidance, especially in 'valley' seasons.
Challenges and attacks often come when there is anointing on your life, but your secure position in Christ means the enemy cannot ultimately defeat you.

versesMentioned.text: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. The life I now live in the body, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.

Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say: 'Now have come the salvation and the power and the kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Messiah. For the accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them before our God day and night, has been hurled down.'

Monday.scripture: Galatians 2:20 (NIV) - 'I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me.'
Monday.action: Begin your day by declaring out loud, 'Christ lives in me.' Carry this truth with you as your source of hope.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that my hope is not based on my circumstances, but on the living Christ inside me. Help me to live from this reality today.

Tuesday.scripture: Colossians 3:3 (NIV) - 'For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.'
Tuesday.action: When you face a challenge today, pause and remind yourself, 'My life is hidden with Christ in God. I am safe in Him.'
Tuesday.prayer: Father, I thank you that I am hidden and secure in you. No attack can touch my true, eternal identity in Christ. I rest in your protection.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 (NIV) - 'Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.'
Wednesday.action: Identify one area where you feel you are in a 'valley' or transition. Write down one reason you can trust that God is still your shepherd in this season.
Wednesday.prayer: Shepherd God, even when I don't understand the path, I choose to trust that you are with me. You are the same God in the valley as you are on the mountain.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 3:18 (NIV) - 'And we all, who with unveiled faces contemplate the Lord’s glory, are being transformed into his image with ever-increasing glory, which comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.'
Thursday.action: Refuse to compare your life, ministry, or progress with someone else's today. Thank God for one unique aspect of your own journey.
Thursday.prayer: God, you are writing a unique story with my life. Forgive me for comparing. I embrace the calling and anointing you have for me alone.

Friday.scripture: Revelation 12:10 (NIV) - '...the accuser of our brothers and sisters... accuses them before our God day and night...'
Friday.action: If a thought of shame, guilt, or 'not being enough' comes to mind today, recognize it as an accusation and reject it in Jesus' name.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, thank you that your blood silences every accusation. I receive your forgiveness and grace right now. I am your forgiven child.

Saturday.scripture: 1 John 4:4 (NIV) - 'You, dear children, are from God and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.'
Saturday.action: Speak this truth over your coming week: 'Because Christ lives in me, trials cannot stop me, and demons cannot defeat me.'
Saturday.prayer: Almighty God, I step into the week ahead not in my own strength, but in the power of Christ who lives in me. You fight my battles. I trust in you.
